essential_question: What's actually different between how you first learned this and what you know now?
objective: Reread the concept note and redo the Lesson Practice / Systematic Review pages for the one lesson (11–19) flagged as shaky, fixing the specific misconception rather than just the wrong answer.

content.heading: Rebuilt, not re-rolled
bullets: Yesterday you picked one lesson (11–19) to reteach.
Today: reread its concept note, then redo its Lesson Practice / Systematic Review pages.
The goal isn't a right answer — it's finding and fixing the actual misconception.

content.heading: Go back to the source
content.note: Find your lesson's concept note: L11→W13-S1, L12→W14-S1, L13→W15-S1, L14→W16-S1, L15→W17-S1, L16→W18-S1, L17→W19-S1, L18→W20-S1, L19→W21-S1 (all under Curriculum/Geometry/Lessons/). Reread it slowly, and rewatch the matching Math-U-See DVD segment if the workbook alone isn't enough.

content.heading: Try the one matching your weak lesson — or all four for extra practice

content.preview: Session 3: reassess — a fresh mastery check across Lessons 11–19.

success_criteria.text: I can redo practice problems from my weak lesson correctly, using the concept note to fix the specific gap
I can describe what changed between my first attempt and this reteach — not just that I got it right, but why

questions.prompt: (L18 — The Pythagorean Theorem) A triangle has sides 9, 12, and 15. Use the Pythagorean converse to show whether it's a right triangle.
questions.answer: Yes — 9²+12²=81+144=225=15², so it satisfies a²+b²=c² and is a right triangle (a 3-4-5 triangle scaled by 3).
questions.explain: The converse says if a²+b²=c² for the two shorter sides and the longest side, the triangle is right.

questions.prompt: (L12 — Circles) The distance from a circle's center to any point on the circle is its…
questions.explain: The radius is the fixed distance from center to the circle itself; the diameter is twice that.

questions.prompt: (L14/L15 — Volume) A cylinder has radius 3 and height 10. Find its volume in terms of π.
questions.explain: Volume of a cylinder is π times radius squared times height.

questions.prompt: Reflect: which of the four redo problems above matched the lesson you're reteaching? What is different this time versus when you first tried it?
